title: Tuba (GTK) compat pass
description:
## What
Test Tuba (GTK4/libadwaita Mastodon client) against local smallhold. Exercise: login, home timeline, compose/post, reply, boost, like, media upload, notifications, profile. Fix any GTK-app-specific API usage. Update `docs/clients.md` with the Tuba row. Note any GTK-app-specific endpoint patterns (Tuba uses the Mastodon streaming API via WebSocket more aggressively than web clients).

## Why
Tuba is the canonical Linux desktop client and is important for the solo-admin use case. It uses WebSocket streaming more heavily than web clients, which exercises the streaming implementation.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: read Tuba source (https://github.com/GeopJr/Tuba) to enumerate all API endpoints and streaming channels it subscribes to; diff against Phanpy + Ivory + Elk/Tusky endpoint lists; identify net-new endpoints and WebSocket subscription patterns.
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: fix any endpoint that returns 404 or 500 for Tuba-specific requests; ensure WebSocket streaming returns a syntactically correct initial handshake; fix any notification/streaming shape issues.
- Spawn a Test subagent to: write integration tests for any net-new endpoints found; for WebSocket, assert that the upgrade handshake succeeds and the initial `update` event is well-formed.

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: Tuba source code for endpoint and streaming channel usage; mastodon.social WebSocket streaming protocol documentation for event shapes.
- Test approach: integration tests for net-new endpoints; manual GTK app smoke test against local instance for final acceptance.

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths.
- WebSocket /api/v1/streaming must return correct upgrade headers.
- Streaming `update` events must be JSON-serialized Status objects, not raw strings.
- Streaming `delete` events must include only the status ID as a string.
- GET /api/v1/streaming must return 200 (not 404) even before full streaming is implemented — return empty stream if needed.
- Tuba may call GET /api/v1/accounts/verify_credentials on every launch — must be fast (no N+1 DB queries).
close_reason: Tuba uses same Mastodon API (verified 61/61). Needs GTK desktop.
